report_name: Southern Illinois Dermatology Breach Exposes Data of Over 150,000 Patients

threat_actors.name: Insomnia
threat_actors.description: A threat group that claimed responsibility for the data breach at Southern Illinois Dermatology. The group performed a double-extortion attack, exfiltrating data from over 150,000 patients and subsequently leaking the entire dataset on its dark web leak site.
threat_actors.goals: To extort money from victim organizations by stealing sensitive data and threatening to leak it publicly.

attack_patterns.name: Account Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The threat actors likely used native tools such as 'net user' and 'net group' to map the internal network and identify accounts after gaining initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Archive via Utility
attack_patterns.description: Patient data was likely aggregated and compressed into large archive files using a utility like rar.exe in a staging area before exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The attackers exfiltrated large archives of patient data by uploading them to attacker-controlled infrastructure, likely using legitimate cloud storage services to blend in with normal traffic.

tools.name: rar.exe
tools.description: The command-line archiving tool rar.exe was likely used to compress and stage stolen patient data for exfiltration.

tools.name: Net
tools.description: The native Windows Net utility was likely used for network and account discovery, with commands such as 'net user' and 'net group'.

identities.name: Southern Illinois Dermatology
identities.description: A healthcare provider that suffered a data breach in November 2025, resulting in the exfiltration and public leak of over 150,000 patients' data.

infrastructure.name: Insomnia Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site operated by the Insomnia threat group used to publicly leak the stolen data from Southern Illinois Dermatology.

indicators.name: Data Staging with RAR Archiver
indicators.pattern:
title: Data Staging with RAR Archiver
id: 2795c1a8-7d8b-4b1f-9c5e-8a6b3d4e5f6a
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of the rar.exe command-line utility to create password-protected archives, a common TTP for data staging before exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.hipaajournal.com/data-breaches-reported-by-southern-illinois-dermatology-heart-south-cardiovascular-group/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-08
tags:
  - attack.t1560.001
  - attack.collection
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\rar.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - ' a '
      - '-hp'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of RAR for archiving by administrators.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects the use of the rar.exe command-line utility to create password-protected archives, a common TTP for data staging before exfiltration.

indicators.name: Account Discovery with Net Command
indicators.pattern:
title: Account Discovery with Net Command
id: 8b3d4e5f-6a27-4b1f-9c5e-9c5e8a6b3d4e
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of 'net user /domain' command for domain account discovery.
references:
  - https://www.hipaajournal.com/data-breaches-reported-by-southern-illinois-dermatology-heart-south-cardiovascular-group/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-08
tags:
  - attack.t1087.002
  - attack.discovery
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\net.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'user'
      - '/domain'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity.
level: low
indicators.description: Detects the use of 'net user /domain' command for domain account discovery.

indicators.name: Data Exfiltration to Cloud Storage Service
indicators.pattern:
title: Data Exfiltration to Cloud Storage Service
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries for common cloud storage services that can be abused for data exfiltration.
references:
  - https://www.hipaajournal.com/data-breaches-reported-by-southern-illinois-dermatology-heart-south-cardiovascular-group/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-08
tags:
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.exfiltration
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|contains:
      - 'mega.nz'
      - 'mega.io'
      - 'dropbox.com'
  filter:
    Image|endswith: # Add legitimate updaters or sync clients here
      - '\Dropbox.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of these cloud services.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to common cloud storage services like Mega.nz or Dropbox, which can be used for data exfiltration.

relationships.source_name: Data Exfiltration to Cloud Storage Service
